topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a stark intrusion into the otherwise calm afternoon. A profile picture loaded: a grainy, overly-filtered shot of a grinning face I hadn't seen in fifteen years. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly. It was Mark. The architect of so many awkward moments in my youth, the one who thrived on making others feel small. I didn't even bother clicking to view the profile. A slow, deliberate exhale escaped my lips, tasting like stale coffee. I minimized the message thread instantly, the action sharp and final. Why would he even reach out now? The thought was irritatingly predictable.

[story 2]

My stomach clenched when the request popped up. I stared at the name, a slow burn starting low in my chest. It was him. The memory of his sneering face, the way he used to loom over me in the hallway, flashed behind my eyes, instantly souring my mood. I leaned back in my chair, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, a defensive posture I didn't realize I was adopting. I tapped the notification once, a small, dismissive gesture, and then deliberately ignored the prompt. It felt like swatting away a persistent, irritating fly.

[story 3]

I picked up my phone, the glass cool against my fingertips, and the sight of the request made my breath catch, not in surprise, but in a deep, unpleasant settling of my features. That smug little face, resurrected online. I let my gaze linger for just a moment too long, cataloging the familiar arrogance in the image. A muscle twitched in my temple. I didn't open the message. Instead, I slid the phone face-down on the desk with a soft, definitive thud, refusing to acknowledge the intrusion whatsoever. The silence that followed felt satisfyingly empty.
